title: stelis emarginata
intro: Stelis emarginata, also known as the 'Emarginate Stelis', is a species of orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its small, white or pale yellow flowers and distinctive lip shape. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to offer health benefits such as relief from respiratory issues, fever reduction, and wound healing due to its bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, Stelis emarginata is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
